skill.name: algorithmic-art
skill.description: Create original generative art with p5.js using seeded randomness, interactive parameters, and reusable HTML viewer templates.
skill.summary: Build reproducible p5.js generative art with seed controls, parameter exploration, and browser-ready artifacts.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are mostly false positives from Apache License text, Markdown backticks, art terminology, and JavaScript regex .exec() usage. The only confirmed risk is public CDN loading for p5.js and Google Fonts in the HTML template; no credential access, command execution, prompt injection, or data exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: External CDN Dependencies in Generated HTML
low_findings.description: The viewer template loads p5.js and Google Fonts from public HTTPS CDNs. This is normal for a browser-based p5.js artifact, but it creates network dependency and supply-chain exposure if the remote resources change or are unavailable.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The HTML template directly references public CDN and font URLs. I found no evidence that these requests include user data or credentials.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many Markdown inline-code and fenced-code examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The reviewed lines are documentation snippets instructing users to read templates or showing p5.js structure, not executable shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged SKILL.md regions are Markdown documentation and JavaScript or HTML examples. There is no shell interpreter invocation or dynamic command construction in those locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: JavaScript Regex exec() Reported as Process Execution
low_findings.description: The generator template uses RegExp.exec() to parse hex color values. This is not child process execution and does not execute user-provided code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code calls a regular expression method on a color string and then parses captured hex values. No process, shell, eval, or Function constructor usage was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: License and Art Terms Reported as Cryptographic or Reconnaissance Signals
low_findings.description: The Apache License URLs and words such as algorithm, system, and particle system triggered weak-cryptography and reconnaissance rules. These are legal text and creative-coding terminology, not security-sensitive operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain license boilerplate, skill metadata, and art system comments. I found no cryptographic implementation, host enumeration, Windows SAM access, or network reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: Creative coding often becomes hard to reproduce, tune, and share. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to build seeded p5.js art with interactive controls and a browser-ready viewer.
actual_capabilities: Creates an algorithmic art philosophy before implementation.
Guides p5.js sketches that use deterministic seeds for reproducible output.
Provides a browser HTML viewer template with seed navigation and controls.
Supports interactive parameter sliders, color controls, reset, regenerate, and PNG download patterns.
Includes a JavaScript reference template for p5.js structure, utility functions, and export behavior.
Encourages original generative systems rather than copying named artists.
limitations: The viewer template loads p5.js and fonts from external CDNs unless modified.
It provides guidance and templates, not a complete art engine for every concept.
Generated sketches still require browser testing for performance and visual quality.
It is focused on p5.js and does not cover WebGL, Three.js, or native desktop rendering.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Gallery-Style Sketch
use_cases.description: Turn a visual concept into a seeded p5.js artwork with adjustable parameters and a shareable browser artifact.

use_cases.title: Build Reproducible Design Variations
use_cases.description: Explore many controlled variations from one algorithm while keeping each result tied to a known seed.

use_cases.title: Teach Generative Art Structure
use_cases.description: Show learners how philosophy, parameters, seeded randomness, and rendering code connect in a complete sketch.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a first generative sketch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an original p5.js generative artwork about flowing light. Include a short algorithmic philosophy and an interactive HTML viewer.

prompt_templates.title: Explore Seeded Variations
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants reproducible outputs and variation controls.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a seeded generative art system with at least five adjustable parameters. Include seed navigation, reset, regenerate, and PNG download controls.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Theme into Code
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a theme but no algorithmic structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Interpret this theme as an algorithmic philosophy, then express it as p5.js code: [describe theme]. Make the reference subtle and original.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Polished Interactive Artifact
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for refined output and strong implementation constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a polished single-file p5.js artifact from the provided viewer template. Use seeded randomness, custom parameters, optimized rendering, and original generative behavior.

output_examples.input: Create generative art based on wind moving through tall grass.
output_examples.output: A short computational philosophy followed by an interactive browser artwork with seed controls, flow parameters, color tuning, and export actions.

output_examples.input: Make 100 reproducible variations of a particle-based composition.
output_examples.output: A seeded p5.js artifact where users can step through seeds, randomize seeds, adjust particle behavior, and download selected outputs.

output_examples.input: Turn a brand mood board into abstract algorithmic art.
output_examples.output: An original generative system that maps the mood board into motion rules, palette controls, density settings, and reproducible visual states.

best_practices: Start with the algorithmic philosophy, then choose particles, fields, or geometry that express it.
Keep every major visual choice tunable through parameters and tied to deterministic seeds.
Test generated HTML in a browser and verify controls, seed behavior, and export actions.
anti_patterns: Do not copy a known artist or recreate a protected artwork directly.
Do not replace the viewer structure with unrelated layout or missing controls.
Do not rely on unbounded particle counts or expensive loops without performance checks.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates original p5.js generative art concepts and single-file interactive HTML artifacts.

faq.question: Does it create static images only?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on algorithms, seed controls, parameter exploration, and browser interaction.

faq.question: Can outputs be reproduced?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill emphasizes seeded randomness so the same seed can recreate the same result.

faq.question: Does it require a web server?
faq.answer: No. The intended artifact is a single HTML file that can open in a browser.

faq.question: Can I adjust the generated artwork?
faq.answer: Yes. The viewer pattern includes sliders, inputs, color controls when useful, and regenerate actions.

faq.question: What should I review before publishing an output?
faq.answer: Check originality, performance, external CDN use, responsive layout, and whether all controls work.
